title: Neelkantha (Nilkantheswar)
tagline: Neelkantha (Nilkantheswar) — spiritual town of Madhya Pradesh

overview.short: Nilkanth Temple or Imarat-i-Dilkhusha (the heart-pleasing abode) is a Mughal architecture temple built on the orders of Mughal Emperor Akbar by the governor of Mandu, Shah Badgah i
overview.description: Nilkanth Temple or Imarat-i-Dilkhusha (the heart-pleasing abode) is a Mughal architecture temple built on the orders of Mughal Emperor Akbar by the governor of Mandu, Shah Badgah in 1574 AD. Akbar commissioned this temple for his favourite wife, Empress Mariam-uz-Zamani, who was a follower of Hinduism. The temple is dedicated to Lord Shiva and is a popular Hindu pilgrimage site. It is surrounded by dense valleys and is adjacent to the mountain ranges of Nilkanth valleys.

nearestAirport.name: Devi Ahilya Bai Holkar Airport, Indore

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Lohani Caves
topPlaces.description: Lohani Caves are rock-cut caves and temples found near the abandoned Mandu site in southwest Madhya Pradesh, India. These excavations are dated to about the 11th and 12th century CE. The archaeological studies have yielded Hindu statues and carvings such as those of Shiva, Parvati, Vishnu and Lakshmi.

topPlaces.name: Jama Masjid, Mandu
topPlaces.description: The Jama Masjid, also called Jami Masjid, is an historic Friday mosque in Mandu in the Dhar district of the state of Madhya Pradesh, India. Built in Mughal style, the mosque has been believed to have been built during the reign of Hoshang Shah and completed during the reign of Mahmud Khilji in 1454 CE. The Masjid has three large domes, a courtyard, 54 smaller domes and colonnade of pillared halls.

topPlaces.name: Mandu, Madhya Pradesh
topPlaces.description: Mandu or Mandavgad is an ancient city in the present-day Mandav area of the Dhar district. It is located in the Malwa and Nimar region of western Madhya Pradesh, India, at 35 km from Dhar city.

topPlaces.name: Mandav
topPlaces.description: Mandav is a town and a nagar panchayat, near city of Dhar in Dhar district in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Hindola Mahal
topPlaces.description: The Hindola Mahal (English translation: “Swinging Palace”), is a large meeting hall, or durbar, in the ancient Indian city of Mandu, Madhya Pradesh. Today the Hindola Mahal is a tourist destination in the ruined city.

topPlaces.name: Diesel Loco Shed, Mhow
topPlaces.description: Diesel Loco Shed, Mhow is a metre-gauge engine shed located in Mhow, in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh. It is located to south of Mhow railway station, it falls under the Ratlam railway division of Western Railway.

topPlaces.name: Nalchha, Dhar
topPlaces.description: Nalchha also spelled as Nalcha is a small town in Dhar district in the state of Madhya Pradesh, India. It is situated 25 km away from Dhar, which is both district & sub-district headquarter of Nalchha village.

The archaeological studies have yielded Hindu statues and carvings such as those of Shiva, Parvati, Vishnu and Lakshmi.

Built in Mughal style, the mosque has been believed to have been built during the reign of Hoshang Shah and completed during the reign of Mahmud Khilji in 1454 CE. The Masjid has three large domes, a courtyard, 54 smaller domes and colonnade of pillared halls.
Mandu or Mandavgad is an ancient city in the present-day Mandav area of the Dhar district. It is located in the Malwa and Nimar region of western Madhya Pradesh, India, at 35 km from Dhar city.
Mandav is a town and a nagar panchayat, near city of Dhar in Dhar district in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh.

The Hindola Mahal (English translation: “Swinging Palace”), is a large meeting hall, or durbar, in the ancient Indian city of Mandu, Madhya Pradesh. Today the Hindola Mahal is a tourist destination in the ruined city.

Diesel Loco Shed, Mhow is a metre-gauge engine shed located in Mhow, in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh. It is located to south of Mhow railway station, it falls under the Ratlam railway division of Western Railway.

Nalchha also spelled as Nalcha is a small town in Dhar district in the state of Madhya Pradesh, India. It is situated 25 km away from Dhar, which is both district & sub-district headquarter of Nalchha village.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Neelkantha (Nilkantheswar)?
faq.a: The best time to visit Neelkantha (Nilkantheswar) is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

The nearest airport is Devi Ahilya Bai Holkar Airport, Indore (61 km) and the nearest railway station is Vadodara Junction (227 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Neelkantha (Nilkantheswar)?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Neelkantha (Nilkantheswar) at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Neelkantha (Nilkantheswar)?
faq.a: Stays in Neelkantha (Nilkantheswar) start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Neelkantha (Nilkantheswar)?
faq.a: The top attractions are Lohani Caves, Jama Masjid, Mandu, Mandu, Madhya Pradesh, Mandav, Hindola Mahal.
